description: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was a Jewish-American street gang based in Manhattan's Lower East Side, formed and headed by Bugsy Siegel and Meyer Lansky during their teenage years shortly after Prohibition. The gang engaged in murder, gambling, bootlegging, theft, racketeering, and extortion, and acted as a predecessor to Murder, Inc. Lansky was considered the brains, while Siegel was the brawn. The mob was active from the 1920s to the 1930s.
